name: Week 12 Pull — Form-First (Spotlight: Face Pull + Reverse Fly)
description: Third week of the form-first cycle. Spotlights rotate to **face pull + reverse fly** — both posterior-shoulder lifts that pair naturally with the right-shoulder rehab work on push days. **NEW (W12D1 discussion):** behind-back DB shrugs added as a final block — see `docs/notes/functional-bulk/shoulder-shrugs-discussion.md` for the full reasoning. The grip → shoulder pain pattern responds to controlled load on the trap chain; W12D1 post-session shrugs already relaxed the right shoulder. **W11D3 takeaways baked in:** pull-ups stepped up to 7/7/7/6 (clean) on adjustable handles → flat bar wide → flat bar close → wide slow; chest-supported row dropped to 30 (35 N/A); DB reverse fly dropped to 20 (22.5 N/A — none of the weights around); lat pulldown forced to dual pulley 70/side ≈ 140 (regular lat pulldown N/A); high cable curl 150 cable column 'felt good, could go up'; cable crunch 120 'felt a bit easy.' The forced equipment subs are noted; if the prescribed setups are free this week, use them. Equipment-consolidated layout holds.

dayOverview.theme: Face pull + reverse fly in the spotlight — both posterior-shoulder lifts that pair with the right-shoulder rehab work. Pull-ups step up to 6-8 reps after W11's clean 7/7/7/6. High cable curl + cable crunch get a small push (W11 said both had headroom).

spotlights.why: W11D3 hit 42.5 x 20 with 'last 5 of last 2 sets hard but finished with ok form' — that's the right state. The cue is the progression now: drill elbows-high + external rotation + 1s peak hold. The lift exists for the rotation, not the volume.

W11D3 was forced to 20lb (22.5 N/A) — 'lighter but somehow never easier, could complete all sets slowly though, still a struggle at end.' Lighter load + clean form = the form-first thesis. W12 drills the seated bent-over + lead-with-elbow at 20-22.5 (whatever's available).

dayOverview.rotationPlan: W10 pull-up + DB row · W11 chest-supported row + lat pulldown · W12 face pull + reverse fly (current) · W13 preacher curl + high cable curl + cable crunch · W14 cycle back to pull-up + DB row.
watchpoints: **Forced equipment subs from W11D3:** chest-supported row 30 (35 N/A), DB reverse fly 20 (22.5 N/A), lat pulldown 70/side dual pulley (regular machine N/A). If the prescribed setup is available this week, use it; if not, the W11 numbers stand. **These were availability subs, not preferences.**
**Left bicep weaker than right** (W10D3 + W11D3 incline curl: right 10 strict, left 5 + assist). Hit left first, freshest reps to the weak side. Stop at strict failure — don't assist past asymmetry.
**~15lb recent weight gain** is making pull-ups harder than W7's 32. W11D3 hit 7/7/7/6 clean — that's the new W12 floor, push to 6-8 with mixed grips.
**High cable curl + cable crunch had headroom** — W11D3 said 150 'could go up,' 120 'felt a bit easy.' Small push this week, NOT a jump.

**NEW: behind-back DB shrugs as the final block** — W12D1 discussion outcome. The grip → right-shoulder pain pattern responds to controlled load on the trap chain. Light to moderate weight, 2-3s top hold, slow eccentric, 'down + back, not up.' See `docs/notes/functional-bulk/shoulder-shrugs-discussion.md`. Stop and report if shrugs WORSEN the grip-shoulder coupling — that flips the working hypothesis from functional to structural and means PT eval.

exercises.notes: Scap activation. Squeeze shoulder blades together, soft elbow.
Spine warm — slow, full ROM each direction.
Active hang, not collapsed — engage lats, pull shoulders DOWN away from ears even at full extension.

**LOAD:** Bodyweight. **Reps adjusted UP from W11's 5-7** — W11D3 hit 7/7/7/6 clean, the floor moved up. 6-8 strict; stop the set when form breaks, never kip.

**Mix grips across sets (W11D3 sequence worked):**
- Set 1: adjustable rack handles (the 'climber-style' handles that don't actually rotate — adjustable, not climber)
- Set 2: flat bar, **wide** grip
- Set 3: flat bar, **close** grip
- Set 4: wide slow — go for clean reps, stop when the rep stops being clean

**Form cues:**
- Active hang start (not collapsed)
- Pull elbows DOWN to ribs, NOT chin to bar
- Chest leads to bar at the top
- Eyes ahead, head neutral — no chin tucking
- Slow eccentric, ~2-3s, return to active hang

**W11D3 watch:** front of right shoulder lit up on the wide grip — stretches help (used to be rotator/scalp, all connected). If it talks this week, drop the wide grip set and replace with another close or neutral set.

sets.name: 🔆 Station 2: DB Rack / Bench Block (Reverse Fly Spotlight + Chest-Supported Row + DB Row + Incline Curl)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** **35lb if available** (prescribed default), 30lb if 35s aren't around (W11D3 forced sub — 'hard doing these slow up to 12 x 3 sets, really tight at end'). 3 sets.
- **Chest stays GLUED to the bench** — if it lifts off, the weight is too heavy
- Pull elbow toward HIP, not toward armpit (toward armpit = rear delt; toward hip = lat)
- Both elbows pull together — asymmetric = slow down
- 2s eccentric, 1s squeeze
- No DB slam at the bottom

**LOAD:** Pyramid 60x10, 70x10, 80x8 (HOLD — W11D3 hit the pyramid; 80 'really hard, kind of broke slow form, more momentum naturally'). Spotlight was W10; sharp cues only.
- Chest-supported on incline bench
- Pull elbow toward HIP, not armpit
- Don't rotate the torso
- Slow 2s descent, 1s squeeze

**W11D3 form watch at 80:** form broke from naturally taking momentum. If 80 still goes ugly this week, hold 75 instead — 80 with clean form > 80 with momentum.

**LOAD:** 25lb (HOLD — W7+ proven swap from preacher; preacher curl bench is always taken at Arrilaga). 3 sets.
- Sit on incline bench (~45-60°), back against the bench, DBs hanging at sides
- **Full stretch at the bottom — pause 2s** (W11D3 user cue)
- Curl smoothly to peak squeeze
- 2-3s eccentric — fight the long-head stretch

**Left first today** — W10D3 + W11D3 both showed left bicep weaker (right got 10 strict, left got 5-7 + assist). Freshest reps to the weak side. Stop the set at strict failure — don't assist past asymmetry.

**Swap:** preacher curl at 12.5/side if it's actually free — usually not.

**LOAD:** **22.5lb if available**, 20lb if not (W11D3 forced sub — 'none of the fucking weights are around'). 3 sets.

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill these every rep:**
- **Seated bent-over** — chest toward thighs (W3 default form). Rounding the upper back instead of hinging at the hips kills the lift.
- **DBs hanging fully extended at start, soft elbow ~10-15°, FIXED throughout** — locked elbow recruits lats, not rear delts.
- **Lead with the elbow, hand passive** — same pattern as lateral raise. Wrist follows.
- **Wings out and slightly UP to shoulder height** — sweep, don't lift. The 'slightly up' is the rear delt; pure horizontal is more middle trap.
- **Slow controlled both directions** — 2s eccentric, 1s squeeze at peak. No swing, no momentum.
- **Breath:** out on lift, in on descent.

**Reflection:** does 20lb feel different at rep 12 of set 3 with the strict bent-over form than W11D3's 'lighter but somehow never easier'? If the lift gets harder as the form gets cleaner, that's the spotlight working — load goes up later, after the cue is automatic.

**Setup option:** reverse-incline (chest-down on incline bench) is even better when the bench is free — removes the awkward seated hunch.

sets.notes: **One trip to the DB rack covers all 4 lifts.** Either circuit (A→B→C→D, rest, repeat) or straight sets (3 of A, then 3 of B). Bench at incline ~30° for the rows + incline curl; sit upright (or use reverse-incline if bench is free) for the reverse fly.

exercises.equipment: regular lat pulldown (default, 140) — dual pulley fallback (70/side ≈ 140 per W11D3 calibration)
exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** regular lat pulldown 140 (HOLD — W10D3 working number). **Forced sub from W11D3:** dual pulley 70/side ≈ 140 (user calibration: 'it's like half the weight, so 70 is 140 more or less'). 3 sets.

**Spotlight was W11; sharp cues only:**
- **Wide grip near the bend** — more lat isolation, less bicep
- **Slight backward lean ~15°, NO further** — more = becomes a row
- **Pull bar to UPPER CHEST — NEVER behind the neck**
- 'Lats squeezing oranges into back pockets' at the bottom
- 1s pause at contraction, 2s controlled return to full overhead stretch
- Knees pinned firmly under pad

**Set count cut from 4 to 3** — W11D3 ran 4 sets at 140 and high cable curl was the lift that 'felt pretty good could go up.' Trim a set here, redirect intensity to the curl + crunch finisher.

**LOAD:** cable column **42.5** (HOLD — W11D3 'last 5 of last 2 sets hard but finished with ok form'). The machine doesn't have 45; 42.5 is the right working number. 3 sets.

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill these every rep:**
- **Pull rope to face level** — not to chest (becomes a row, loses rear delt focus)
- **ELBOWS HIGH (above shoulders)** — this is the make-or-break cue. If elbows drop below shoulders, the rear delt offloads and the lift becomes a chest-supported pull.
- **External rotate at the top — knuckles point UP, like a double-biceps pose.** This is why the lift exists — for the rotation, not the volume. No external rotation = no shoulder-health benefit, just back work.
- **1s peak hold at the contracted position** — count it. The hold is where the rear delt + external rotators get loaded longest.
- **Slow controlled return, 2s** — let the cable pull the arms back. Speed up the return = lose the stretch stimulus.
- **Breath:** out on pull, in on return.

**Reflection:** at set 3, are the elbows still ABOVE the shoulders, or have they crept down to shoulder height? If they've dropped, the rear delts have tapped out — stop the set. The lift's job is shoulder rotation health; doing it sloppy doesn't add anything.

**Setup: pick the cable column you'll use for the rest of the workout** — high cable curl + cable crunch live at the same column. Slide the rope from the chest-height attachment to the high attachment after this exercise.

sets.name: Station 5: Cable Column — Finisher (High Cable Curl + Cable Crunch)

exercises.equipment: cable column (high attachment) — same column as face pull
exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** cable column **160** (small push — W11D3 hit 150 each side x 2, 'felt pretty good, could go up'). One arm at a time. 2 sets each side.
- **Single D-handle, one arm at a time** (the W11D3 setup that 'felt nice')
- Stand sideways, arm out at shoulder level
- **Upper arm parallel to floor, FIXED** — if elbow drops, becomes a regular curl
- Curl hand to ear, peak squeeze
- Slow eccentric, no bounce at start

**Push to 170 only if 160 feels like W11's 150** — same 'felt good, could go up' state. Otherwise hold 160.

**LOAD:** cable column **130** (small push — W11D3 hit 120 'felt a bit easy'). 2 sets.
- **High attachment + rope** (swap from D-handle if needed — same column)
- Kneel under the high cable
- Rope behind head, **HIPS LOCKED** (most important cue)
- **Crunch from the abs only** — spine flexes, hips don't move. Hips moving = hip flexor work, not abs.
- Chest toward thighs, hold the crunch 1 second
- Slow eccentric

**If 130 feels like W11's 120 ('a bit easy'), push to 140 next week.**

sets.name: Station 6: Shoulder Reset — Behind-Back DB Shrugs (NEW, W12D1 discussion outcome)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** Start **30-40lb DBs** — light to moderate, this is an introduction. Build the cue first, the load comes later. 3 sets.

**Why this lift, this week** (full reasoning in `docs/notes/functional-bulk/shoulder-shrugs-discussion.md`):
- W12D1 surfaced a grip-squeeze → shoulder pain pattern + 'super tight' shoulders + tight neck + tight right pec, all as a unit.
- Loading helps (cable fly stretch, shrugs both relaxed the shoulder). That's the signal that the pattern is **functional**, not structural — upper traps locked tonic, lower/mid trap underused.
- Behind-the-back variant **biases lower trap + mid trap** vs. front shrugs which are upper-trap dominant. Lower trap is the underused side of the pattern.

**Cues (drill these every rep):**
- **DBs held BEHIND the body**, palms facing back, arms straight
- **Ribs DOWN** before the first rep — no rib flare under the shrug
- **Pull the shoulder blades into your back pockets at the top, NOT up to your ears.** Down + back, not up. The cue for this lift is opposite the cue for a standard front shrug.
- **2-3s hold at the top** — count it. The hold is where the lower trap loads.
- **Slow eccentric**, controlled all the way down. No bounce.
- Don't roll the shoulders — straight up + back, straight down.

**What to watch (REPORT BACK):**
- Does the grip → right-shoulder coupling **reduce** after these? (Hypothesis: yes — that's why we're adding them.)
- If the coupling **worsens** under shrugs → the structural side (cervical, TOS) is the real driver. Stop the protocol and book a PT eval.
- Right pec / neck tightness should also respond. If it doesn't shift over 2-3 sessions, escalate to PT.

**Rotation:** alternates with `trap-3-raise` across pull days going forward — W13D3 swaps to trap-3 raises (more lower-trap isolation, lighter load). Don't do both same day.

sets.notes: Quick block at the DB rack on the way out. 3 sets, light load, focus is the cue. Total ~4 min including rest.

sets.name: Cool-Down — 5 Min Fascia/Recovery Block (NEW from W12D1 discussion)

exercises.notes: 30s each arm at MID elbow position. Arm bent 90°, forearm on door frame, step through + rotate torso AWAY. Pec minor lengthen — the antagonist to the upper-trap chain that's been tonically loaded all session.
Passive hang from the pull-up bar (or any sturdy overhead). **Don't engage** — let the shoulders unpack and the spine decompress. The opposite of every loaded lift today.
30s each side on the mat. Lie back, draw one knee across the body, opposite arm extended. Breathe into the rib cage on the rotated side.
45s each side. Front leg + back leg at 90°. Sink hips toward the floor. Hip-rotator decompression — pulls the QL/glute pattern out of the squat/deadlift bracing.
60s slow breathing. Knees wide, big toes touching, arms extended forward. Breathe into the back ribs — parasympathetic shift before leaving the gym.

sets.notes: **5 minutes of recovery work, non-negotiable closing block (W12D1 discussion outcome).** Targets the four chains that get hammered in gym work: pec minor (doorframe), shoulder decompression (dead hang), thoracic rotation (supine twist), hip rotators (90/90), parasympathetic reset (child's pose). Goes after the last lift, before walking out. This is what catches the connective-tissue debt that builds across hard training weeks.

tips: **Spotlights: face pull + reverse fly.** Both posterior-shoulder lifts that pair with the push-day shoulder rehab. Drill the cues, hold the loads.
**Face pull: elbows HIGH + external rotation IS the lift.** Without those cues it's just a row.
**Reverse fly: lighter + cleaner > heavier + sloppy.** W11D3 'lighter but somehow never easier' is the form-first thesis in one sentence.
**Pull-ups bumped to 6-8** after W11's clean 7/7/7/6. Mix grips: rack handles → flat bar wide → flat bar close → wide slow.

**Forced equipment subs from W11D3** carry forward as fallbacks: chest-supported row 30 (35 if available), reverse fly 20 (22.5 if available), lat pulldown dual pulley 70/side (regular machine 140 if available).
**Left bicep weak side — left first.** Strict reps only, no assist past asymmetry.
**High cable curl 160 + cable crunch 130** — small pushes, NOT jumps. W11 said both had headroom.
**DB row pyramid 60/70/80 holds** — but if 80 still breaks slow form like W11, drop to 75 instead. Clean > heavy.

**NEW closing block: behind-back DB shrugs 30-40lb x 12 x 3.** W12D1 discussion outcome — the grip → shoulder pattern responds to loaded trap chain work. Cue is 'down + back, not up.' See `docs/notes/functional-bulk/shoulder-shrugs-discussion.md`. **Report if it worsens the coupling** — that means PT eval.
Set count trim: lat pulldown 4→3 sets, redirected intensity to face pull + curl/crunch finisher.
Rotation plan: W13 preacher curl + high cable curl + cable crunch · W13 closing block rotates to trap-3 raises · W14 cycle back to pull-up + DB row.
Rest 2.5 min pull-ups, 90s DB block (4 lifts circuit), 2 min lat pulldown, 90s face pull, 60s curl/crunch finisher, 60s closing shrug block.
formPhilosophy: Form-first base-building, week 3 of the cycle. Same loads as W11D3 with sharper cues on face pull + reverse fly. Pull-ups step up to 6-8 because last week's 7/7/7/6 was clean. Small pushes on the lifts that had headroom (high cable curl, cable crunch). The cue is still the progression.
